slides.prompt:
STYLE — Korean handwritten note-style presentation slide, 16:9 landscape:
- Background: warm cream paper (#FCF8F2), subtle paper texture, no lines/grid.
- Text: neat black felt-tip pen Korean handwriting (매직펜 손글씨), thick confident
  strokes, consistent stroke weight throughout. Titles large and bold; body text
  medium. All Korean text must be rendered VERBATIM as quoted — every syllable
  exactly as written, no invented characters.
- Accents: blue (#2B4FD8) double underline below the title; yellow highlighter
  (#FFE9A8) behind 1-3 keywords only; red (#D8342B) hand-drawn doodles (small stars,
  sparkle ticks) used sparingly at corners.
- Cards: rounded rectangles with hand-drawn black outline (slightly wobbly, like
  drawn with a pen). An emphasized card may use a green or red outline.
- Mascot: small cute line-drawn robot (round head, antenna with red dot, simple
  smiling face) — include only when the layout recipe says so.
- Numbers and Latin text (e.g. "88%", "METR", "AI") in the same handwritten style.
- Clean composition, generous margins, nothing touching the edges.

LAYOUT — Cover slide:
- Very large English title in handwritten style, top-left area, 1-2 lines:
  "We are 1% done"
- Blue double underline sweeping under the title.
- Subtitle row of 3 short phrases separated by middle dots, each with yellow
  highlighter: "AI 구루들의 목소리" · "2026 여름 필기노트" · "에이전트의 미래"
- Bottom-left byline in smaller handwriting: "AI 세미나  ·  2026년 7월"
- Bottom-right: waving robot mascot with a speech bubble saying "필기해 왔어요!"
- One red doodle star near the title.

STRICT: No page number anywhere on the slide. Do not draw any text that is not
specified above. Render every quoted string VERBATIM — every syllable and every
English word exactly as written.

LAYOUT — Single-message slide:
- Large 2-line English statement in the center, in quotation marks:
  "I don't think I've typed like a line of code probably since December."
  with yellow highlighter on "a line of code", blue double underline beneath.
- Directly below, one smaller attribution line in handwriting:
  "— Andrej Karpathy · 前 Tesla AI 리드 · 2026. 3"
- One rounded banner lower on the slide with the supporting line:
  "그럼 우리는, 오늘 몇 줄을 썼을까?"
- Robot mascot bottom-right looking curious; sparse, lots of cream space.

STRICT: No page number anywhere on the slide. Do not draw any text that is not
specified above. Render every quoted string VERBATIM — every syllable and every
English word exactly as written.

LAYOUT — Stacked cards:
- Title at top: "오늘의 등장인물" with blue double underline.
- 5 rounded-rectangle cards stacked vertically. Each card: left side a name label
  with yellow highlighter, a thin vertical divider, right side one short line:
  1. "Boris Cherny" | "Claude Code 만든 사람 — 100줄이면 된다"
  2. "Andrej Karpathy" | "前 Tesla AI 리드 — 에이전트에게 연구를 시켰다"
  3. "Demis Hassabis" | "Google DeepMind CEO — 브레이크를 설계하자"
  4. "Simon Willison" | "Django 같이 만든 사람 — 보안 경보"
  5. "Armin Ronacher · Mario Zechner" | "Flask · Pi 만든 사람들 — 냉정한 반론"
- Bottom: a punchline banner (rounded rect, red outline):
  "낙관, 냉소, 경고 — 다 적어왔습니다"
- Small robot mascot peeking from the right edge. One red sparkle doodle.

  strokes, consistent stroke weight throughout. Titles large and bold; body text
  medium. All Korean text must be rendered VERBATIM as quoted — every syllable
  exactly as written, no invented characters.

  (#FFE9A8) behind 1-3 keywords only; red (#D8342B) hand-drawn doodles (small stars,
  sparkle ticks) used sparingly at corners.
- Cards: rounded rectangles with hand-drawn black outline (slightly wobbly, like
  drawn with a pen). An emphasized card may use a green or red outline.
- Mascot: small cute line-drawn robot (round head, antenna with red dot, simple
  smiling face) — include only when the layout recipe says so.
- Numbers and Latin text (e.g. "88%", "METR", "AI") in the same handwritten style.
- Clean composition, generous margins, nothing touching the edges.
